title: WelQrate Small-Molecule Drug Discovery Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Project page links downloads, curation source code, and experiment source code.
Use paper for curation and split/evaluation semantics.

instructions: Use the official WelQrate download or package and pin dataset version.
Record assay ID, molecule identifier, SMILES/InChIKey, activity label, split scheme, and curation source.
Treat WelQrate's split schemes as operating points and construct SPECTRA curves over chemical and assay-support axes.

scale.download_size: moderate curated benchmark collection; full official release should be feasible after version pinning

recommended_axes.name: chemical_structure_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Morgan fingerprint Tanimoto, scaffold identity, or chemical descriptor similarity to training compounds.

recommended_axes.name: assay_and_target_support
recommended_axes.definition: Assay, target, or screen-source support when metadata are available.

recommended_axes.name: activity_prevalence_and_hit_rate
recommended_axes.definition: Training-support distance for low-hit-rate screening settings; diagnostic unless based only on train labels.

useful_for: Drug-discovery SPECTRA audits focused on realistic virtual-screening evaluation.
Testing chemical novelty versus assay/source curation effects.
Comparing WelQrate split schemes with full SPECTRA chemical-support curves.
leakage_risks: Molecule duplicates and salt/stereochemistry canonicalization can leak across splits.
Assay curation and confirmatory/counter-screen information can leak label semantics if used incorrectly.
Hit-rate imbalance can dominate metrics and must be reported with split statistics.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified WelQrate as a curated small-molecule drug-discovery benchmark; official source reports nine datasets, downloads, and split/evaluation framework.
